Client.Name: 国立遺伝学研究所 (National Institute of Genetics, Japan)

Project.Brief history of your project and organi:
PROJECT
NextGen Gene Center has been conducting applied genomics research focused on large-scale comparative sequence analysis and computational modeling of genomic variation. Over the past few years, the team has increasingly relied on public nucleotide sequence repositories such as DNA Data Bank of Japan to support cross-species genome alignment, reference validation, and benchmarking of internal bioinformatics pipelines. One of the research scenarios involves integrating publicly released DDBJ sequence datasets with internally generated analytical models to evaluate sequence patterns across multiple organisms. While the public datasets themselves are open, the downstream analytical frameworks, model architectures, and research objectives are subject to confidentiality agreements and cannot be described in detail.

ORGANIZATION
NextGen Gene Center, Inc. (株式会社新世代遺伝子センター) (Website as https://www.nextgen-gene.center/ ) is revolutionizing healthcare in Japan through cutting-edge medical care. We offer clinical genetic testing with our advanced technology and expert analysis. We empower patients and healthcare professionals with accurate, actionable insights and make informed decisions for personalized treatment plans. Commitment: Striving for excellence, we meet diverse needs and drive the future of genomics.
Project.Describe the data being stored onto File:
The data to be stored consists of publicly accessible nucleotide sequence datasets from DNA Data Bank of Japan (DDBJ).

Scope of data includes:
Assembled DNA and RNA sequence records
Associated annotation files and metadata
Publicly released sequencing read data
Periodic release snapshots for reproducible research archiving

Data characteristics:
Source: Open scientific submissions from global research institutions
Licensing: Publicly accessible research data under INSDC open data policies
Format: Structured biological sequence data

Purpose of storage on Filecoin: (1)Long-term decentralized backup of public genomic data. (2)Improved redundancy and resilience. (3)Public-good preservation use case aligned with open science principles.

No private, clinical, or restricted-access human subject data will be included.
Project.How do you plan to prepare the dataset: Public DDBJ datasets will be programmatically retrieved via official FTP/HTTP endpoints, verified with checksums, normalized into standardized archive batches, and prepared for Filecoin sealing using automated scripts with manifest indexing for CID traceability.
